Motion To Compel Arbitration (Filed As A Demurrer/Re-Characterized In Open Court On February 11, 2016)
Off Calendar Mar-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JENNIFER SOMMER, ESQ. of Gilbert, Kelly, Crowley & Jennett LLP, Tel No. 415-352-6416 appearing for Defendant GEICO;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to Defendant GEICO'S Motion To Compel Arbitration (Filed As A Demurrer/Re-Characterized In Open Court On February 11, 2016), GEICO's recharacterized motion to compel the arbitration of the UM claim and stay the remaining claims pending the conclusion of the arbitration is granted. GEICO has not waived the mandatory arbitration provision in the policy and adjudication of the UM claim should occur before adjudication of plaintiff's other claims because those other claims are dependent on the outcome of the UM claim. Defendant shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature via e-mail to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JENNIFER SOMMER, ESQ. of Gilbert, Kelly, Crowley & Jennett LLP, Tel No. 415-352-6416 appearing for Defendants Government Eeployees Insurance Company and Cecilia Meyers;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES INSURANCE COMPANY AND CECILIA MEYER'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's Complaint is off calendar as moot given the ruling on its demurrer. SINGLE ORDER SIGNED RE: BOTH DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JENNIFER SOMMER, ESQ. of Gilbert, Kelly, Crowley & Jennett LLP, Tel No. 415-352-6416 appearing for Defendants Government Eeployees Insurance Company and Cecilia Meyers;
No other appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES INSURANCE COMPANY AND CECILIA MEYER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, GEICO's demurrer is being treated as a motion to compel arbitration of plaintiff's claim for uninsured motorist benefits under his policy and to stay all of plaintiff's other claims pending the determination of the arbitration. Treating the demurrer in this way is not only faithful to GEICO's principal argument that plaintiff's claims are premature or not ripe until the parties arbitrate the UM claim, but also preserves plaintiff's ability to proceed with his other claims. Because plaintiff has not substantively responded to the argument that he is required to arbitrate his UM claim and has not expressed any view as to whether, if such arbitration is required, his other claims should be stayed pending the arbitration, plaintiff is permitted to file supplemental opposition papers limited to these issues no later than February 22, 2016. GEICO is permitted to file reply papers no later than February 29, 2016. A further hearing will be held on the re-characterized motion on March 7, 2016. SINGLE ORDER SIGNED RE: DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
